pages.raw_text:
SEATTLE, Dec. 13.-A lockout
of the organized building tradesmen
and building laborers employed by
more than hundred of the leading
building contractors of this city
will be precipitated If the employ
ers undertake to carry out reso-
luton which declares for the open
shop and which was adopted by the
Seattle Builders exchange and also
by the Master Builders association.
Should the lockout occur, several
hundred employees will be affected
and the building industry of the
city will be completely tied up, at
least temporarily.


The contractors of Seattle, as
represented by the Master Build
ers' association, signed up with the
labor unions last summer, the con-
tract to hold good for two years.
The unions claim that the contrac-
tors have organized the Seattle
Builders exchange for the purpose
of sidestepping their contract and
precipitating fight with the
unions.


The contractors claim the union
men broke faith with them and
cite the fact that the union brick
layers walked off the federal build
ing in this city on account of the
hiring of a nonunion man, notwith-
standing the union men invariably
demaded the closed shop and the
effort to hire a nonunion bricklay
err was regarded as hostile act on
the part Of the contractors.
